commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P2] Human sandbox/security review is needed because the remaining blocker is the canonical mount precedence contract, not a narrow safe automation repair.; Needs attention: The diff changes sandbox mount-boundary behavior and can let configured binds shadow protected read-only skill roots.; Review finding: [P1] Keep protected skill mounts authoritative — src/agents/sandbox/workspace-mounts.ts:142 - [P2] Make the... (sha=f9f3d5eae136919207c6f70004432803a5a1aa7f)
commands.review_summary:
The branch filters read-only workspace skill mounts against configured Docker/browser binds, threads that filtered list into filesystem path modeling, updates sandbox tests, and adds a sandbox bind-filter demo script.

PR surface: Source +43, Tests +71, Other +81. Total +195 across 8 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main and v2026.6.10 append custom binds before read-only skill mounts in shell and browser Docker create args, so a dangerous reserved-target bind can produce duplicate Docker targets; I did not run a live Docker daemon in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Human sandbox/security review is needed because the remaining blocker is the canonical mount precedence contract, not a narrow safe automation repair.

target.title: fix(sandbox): skip read-only skill mounts when user binds target the same container path
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `openclaw.json` `sandbox.docker.binds` entries targeting reserved container paths (e.g. `/workspace/.agents/skills`) cause the Docker daemon to reject container creation with `Duplicate mount point`. The internal `resolveReadOnlyWorkspaceSkillMounts` unconditionally appends read-only skill mounts without checking whether a user-defined bind already targets the same container path.
- Why now: Any user with custom docker binds that happen to overlap a skill mount path cannot launch sandbox containers at all. The gateway fails before the agent can start.
- What changed:
  - New `filterReadOnlyWorkspaceSkillMountsByBinds()` in `workspace-mounts.ts` parses user bind specs and excludes skill mounts whose container path is already targeted
  - `ensureSandboxContainer` applies the filter before config hash computation and container creation
  - `ensureSandboxBrowser` applies the filter before browser container creation
  - `buildSandboxFsMounts` applies the filter to keep the FS path resolution table consistent
- What did NOT change: Guard mounts in `agent-tools.ts` are unchanged — they don't generate Docker `-v` args. The `appendWorkspaceMountArgs` fallback path is untouched because production callers always pass `includeReadOnlyWorkspaceSkillMounts: false`. No new dependencies added.
- Outcome: User binds that conflict with skill mount paths no longer cause `Duplicate mount point`. User binds take precedence. Config hashes are computed from the effective mount list.
- Reviewer focus: `workspace-mounts.ts` `filterReadOnlyWorkspaceSkillMountsByBinds` function, and the three integration points in `docker.ts`, `browser.ts`, `fs-paths.ts`.

## Linked context

Closes #93854

Was this requested by a maintainer?
- No, but the issue has clear reproduction steps and root cause analysis.

## Root Cause

- Root cause: `resolveReadOnlyWorkspaceSkillMounts` unconditionally appends internal read-only skill mounts for `rw` workspaces. When a user bind targets the same container path, `appendReadOnlyWorkspaceSkillMountArgs` adds a second `-v` spec for the same Docker target, and the daemon rejects the container.
- Missing detection / guardrail: No pre-existing check for container-path overlap between user binds and internal skill mounts. The project's comment-based mount ordering tests assumed Docker would accept duplicate targets and "last one wins", but Docker rejects duplicate mount points.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: `openclaw.json` `sandbox.docker.binds` with `/host/custom-skills:/workspace/skills:rw` must not produce a second `-v ...:/workspace/skills:ro` — that second mount would cause Docker to reject the container with `Duplicate mount point`.
- Real environment tested: Linux x64, Node 22.19.0, pnpm 11.2.2, Docker 20.10.0, branch `fix/sandbox-bind-conflict`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  ```bash
  node --import tsx scripts/e2e-sandbox-bind-conflict.mjs
  pnpm test src/agents/sandbox/
  ```
- Evidence after fix (real Docker container create + docker inspect):
  ```text
  $ node --import tsx scripts/e2e-sandbox-bind-conflict.mjs
  Workspace: /tmp/openclaw-e2e-IGDwq2

  --- Skill mounts ---
  Raw: [ '/tmp/openclaw-e2e-IGDwq2/skills -> /workspace/skills' ]
  Filtered: (none — conflicting mount filtered out)

  --- Docker args ---
    create
    --name
    oc-e2e-bind-1782723336265
    --label
    openclaw.e2e=1
    --workdir
    /workspace
    -v /tmp/openclaw-e2e-IGDwq2:/workspace
    -v /tmp/openclaw-e2e-IGDwq2/custom-mount:/workspace/skills:rw
    e2e-sleep:latest
    infinity

  --- Duplicate check ---
  ✅ No duplicate container paths in -v args

  --- docker create oc-e2e-bind-1782723336265 ---
  ✅ Container created
  Mount destinations: /workspace /workspace/skills
  /workspace/skills count: 1 ✅
  Mount source for /workspace/skills: /tmp/openclaw-e2e-IGDwq2/custom-mount
  Source is custom bind host: ✅
  ```

  ```text
  $ pnpm test src/agents/sandbox/
   Test Files  22 passed (22)
        Tests  224 passed (224)
     Start at  17:40:41
     Duration  9.90s
  ```
- Observed result after fix: The conflicting `/workspace/skills` skill mount is absent from the filtered output. Docker `create` succeeds — no `Duplicate mount point` error. `docker inspect` confirms `/workspace/skills` appears exactly once with the user's custom bind host as the source. All 224 sandbox unit/integration tests pass.
- What was not tested: Live end-to-end with the full OpenClaw gateway and a real agent session. The proof exercises the exact production code path for mount resolution + actual Docker container creation.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?
- `node --import tsx scripts/e2e-sandbox-bind-conflict.mjs` (real Docker container create + inspect)
- `pnpm test src/agents/sandbox/` (224 tests, 22 files)
- `pnpm test src/agents/sandbox/workspace-mounts.test.ts`
- `pnpm test src/agents/sandbox/fs-paths.test.ts`
- `pnpm test src/agents/agent-tools.sandbox-mounted-paths.workspace-only.test.ts`

What regression coverage was added or updated?
- Added 8 new unit tests for `filterReadOnlyWorkspaceSkillMountsByBinds`:
  1. `returns all mounts when binds is undefined`
  2. `returns all mounts when binds is empty`
  3. `filters out a mount whose container path matches a user bind`
  4. `filters out multiple mounts when multiple binds conflict`
  5. `returns all mounts when no bind targets conflict`
  6. `filters out all mounts when every container path is targeted by a bind`
  7. `handles rw binds (no ro option) correctly`
  8. `normalizes container paths before comparing`
- Updated 2 existing integration tests:
  1. `docker.config-hash-recreate.test.ts`: asserts conflicting mount is absent instead of appended last
  2. `browser.create.test.ts`: same update for the browser sandbox path
- Added 1 live proof script: `scripts/demo-sandbox-bind-filter.mjs`

What failed before this fix, if known?
- Docker daemon returned `Duplicate mount point` error when a user bind targeted the same container path as an internal skill mount. Container creation failed entirely — the agent could not start.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
target.author: chenyangjun-xy
